title: 8 Powerful Herbal Tinctures for Motion Sickness and Dizziness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol in PIPER NIGRUM is good for motion sickness because it helps to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea by blocking the action of natural chemicals that cause vomiting.

Shogaol is good for motion sickness because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by preventing the release of certain chemicals in the body that cause these symptoms.
Zingiberene is good for motion sickness because it has a calming effect on the stomach and helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the muscles in the digestive system.
Gingerols are good for motion sickness because they help reduce nausea and vomiting by controlling the activity of brain chemicals that cause these symptoms.
Shogaols are good for motion sickness because they help to reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the action of a chemical in the brain that causes these symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help with motion sickness. This is because ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the digestive system. When you're on a boat, in a car, or on a plane, your body can feel queasy and your stomach can churn. Ginger tincture works by reducing nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness. It also helps to slow down the movement of your digestive system, giving your body a break from the constant feeling of being sick. Additionally, ginger has a soothing effect on the stomach, which can help to ease the discomfort and pain associated with motion sickness. Taking ginger tincture before you travel can help to prevent motion sickness from happening in the first place. It's also a good idea to take it as soon as you start to feel queasy, as it can help to quickly ease your symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal tinctures for motion sickness, first you'll need some black peppercorns and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Start by cleaning and drying the black peppercorns, then place them in a clean glass jar. Next, pour the solvent over the peppercorns, making sure they're completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part black peppercorns to 2 parts solvent. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This will allow the peppercorns to infuse their active compounds into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the peppercorns. Your Piper nigrum tincture is now ready to use, but it's recommended to store it in the fridge and to take a few drops as needed to alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for motion sickness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for motion sickness because it helps reduce inflammation and stabilize blood vessels, which can help prevent the symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea and dizziness.
Ginkgolide B in piper nigrum is good for motion sickness because it blocks the action of certain chemicals in the brain that cause nausea and vomiting when you are in a moving vehicle.
Kaempferol is good for motion sickness because it helps to protect the stomach from the effects of nausea and vomiting by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent.
Bilobalide is good for motion sickness because it helps to stabilize the inner ear and reduce nausea by acting on the brain's neurotransmitters and balancing the body's natural response to motion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is used by some people to help with motion sickness. The tincture is made from the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree, which is known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. When you take the tincture, the active compounds in the Ginkgo biloba leaves may help to improve blood flow to the brain. This can lead to better communication between the inner ear and the brain, which can help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness. The inner ear is responsible for balance and equilibrium, and when it sends signals to the brain, the brain can better cope with the conflicting signals that can cause motion sickness. By improving blood flow and communication between the inner ear and the brain, Ginkgo biloba tincture may help to reduce nausea, dizziness, and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Some people also use Ginkgo biloba tincture to help with vertigo and other balance-related problems, which can be related to motion sickness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal tinctures that help with motion sickness, you will need dried Piper nigrum, a solvent like vodka, a glass jar with a lid, and a dark glass bottle. First, fill the glass jar about one-quarter with the dried Piper nigrum. Next, fill the rest of the jar with the solvent, making sure that the herb is completely covered. Now, close the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day. This will allow the herb to infuse into the solvent and create the tincture. After two weeks, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. You now have a tincture that you can take to help with motion sickness by dropping a few drops under your tongue or adding it to water.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for motion sickness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for motion sickness because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow in the inner ear, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.
Quercetin is good for motion sickness because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, which can ease nausea and vomiting caused by motion.
Tannins are good for motion sickness because they help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can calm nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.
Kaempferol is good for motion sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the stomach muscles and improving blood flow to the brain.
Polyphenols are good for motion sickness because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by balancing the levels of serotonin in the brain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures are often used to help with motion sickness because they contain a chemical called harmine. Harmine helps to calm the brain and body, reducing the feelings of nausea and dizziness that come with motion sickness. When you take Passiflora incarnata tincture, the harmine works by slowing down the messages that are sent to the brain, which can help to stop the feelings of sickness from starting. This makes it easier to travel by car, bus, or boat without getting queasy. Passiflora incarnata tinctures have been used for a long time to help with anxiety and sleep problems, so it's not surprising that it also helps with motion sickness. The tincture is made by mixing dried Passiflora incarnata flowers with a liquid, such as water or vodka, which makes it easy to take as a supplement.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal tincture, start by gathering dried black pepper seeds or 1-2 tablespoons of fresh peppercorns. Add these to a clean glass jar, then fill it with 80-100 proof vodka, gin, or another high-proof spirit, making sure the seeds are completely covered. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called maceration, and it helps to extract the healing properties from the black pepper seeds into the spirit. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms. The recommended dosage is typically 20-30 drops, taken 15-30 minutes before traveling.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal tinctures for motion sickness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins help to prevent motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines, making them a natural remedy to alleviate nausea and vomiting.

Avenacosides are good for motion sickness because they can help reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the action of a chemical in the brain that makes you feel queasy.

Phenolic acids in PIPER NIGRUM are good for motion sickness because they have strong antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and stabilize the digestive system, making them effective in preventing and treating nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Fatty acids, also known as black pepper, have been found to be effective in treating motion sickness due to their ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the stomach, which helps alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tincture is often used to help with motion sickness. This is because it contains a natural chemical that helps calm the brain and body. When you're on a boat or a car, your body gets mixed signals, and this can make you feel queasy and dizzy. Avena sativa tincture works by slowing down your nervous system, which can help reduce the conflicting signals and make you feel more stable. It also has a soothing effect on the stomach, which can help ease nausea and vomiting. Many people find that taking Avena sativa tincture before traveling or during motion can help them feel more comfortable and calm, and reduce the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal tincture that helps with motion sickness, you will need dried Piper nigrum seeds, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper. First, fill the glass jar with the dried Piper nigrum seeds and pour enough solvent over them to cover the seeds completely. Make sure the jar is sealed tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process is called maceration and it helps to extract the active ingredients from the seeds into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. When you need to take it, simply add a few drops to a glass of water or take it directly under the tongue. The Piper nigrum tincture can be taken 30 minutes before traveling to help prevent motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper methysticum, also known as kava, has been used for centuries to treat motion sickness. The tincture, made from the root of the kava plant, is believed to help calm the nervous system and reduce the symptoms of motion sickness. When you take a kava tincture before traveling, the active compounds in the plant, called kavalactones, work to relax the muscles and calm the stomach. This helps to ease the feelings of nausea and dizziness that often come with motion sickness. Additionally, kava has been shown to have a sedative effect on the brain, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress related to travel. By taking a kava tincture before a trip, you may be able to reduce the severity of your motion sickness symptoms and enjoy your journey more comfortably.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Piper nigrum herbal tincture that helps with motion sickness, start by gathering fresh or dried peppercorns from the Piper nigrum plant. If using fresh peppercorns, dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150-200°F (65-90°C) for 2-3 hours or until they are completely dry. Next, combine 1 part of the dried peppercorns with 2-3 parts of a solvent such as vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The solvent helps to extract the active compounds from the peppercorns. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the extraction process. After the steeping period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small amounts as needed to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Piper methysticum herbal tinctures for motion sickness

Valerenal is good for motion sickness because it has natural anti-inflammatory and calming properties that help to reduce nausea and vomiting.

Isovaleric acid is good for motion sickness because it has been found to help prevent nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and stabilizing the stomach's natural balance, making it a potential natural remedy for people who experience motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tincture is a natural remedy for motion sickness. This is because it contains valerenic acid, a compound that helps calm the nervous system. Motion sickness occurs when the body receives conflicting signals from the senses, causing dizziness and nausea. Valeriana officinalis tincture works by soothing the nervous system, reducing the conflicting signals and calming the stomach. This helps to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. The tincture also has a sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. As a result, Valeriana officinalis tincture can be a useful remedy for people who experience motion sickness, especially when traveling by car, boat, or plane. Taking a small dose of the tincture before traveling can help to prevent motion sickness symptoms from arising.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal tinctures that help with motion sickness, start by gathering dried peppercorns and a neutral-tasting liquid like vodka or glycerin. Combine one part dried peppercorns with two parts liquid in a clean glass jar, then seal it tightly. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it every few days to help the peppercorns release their active ingredients into the liquid. After the steeping time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the remaining liquid to small glass bottles and label them. To use the tincture, mix a few drops of it with water and take it before traveling or when you feel motion sickness coming on. Note that you can also add a few drops of the tincture to your tea or food for added relief. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that can help with motion sickness. This tincture is made from the bark of the Cinchona tree and has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. It contains a chemical called quinine, which is known to help reduce nausea and vomiting. When you're on a boat, plane, or car and feel queasy, quinine in the tincture helps calm your stomach and stop the queasiness. It also reduces the release of chemicals in the brain that cause nausea, making it easier to travel without feeling sick. To use Cinchona officinalis tincture for motion sickness, take a few drops in water or another liquid before traveling. It's also a good idea to take it as needed, when you start to feel queasy. This can help you stay calm and focused while traveling, and enjoy your journey without feeling sick.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Piper nigrum herbal tincture that helps with motion sickness, start by gathering a few fresh black peppercorns or 1-2 teaspoons of dried black peppercorns. Combine the peppercorns with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, making sure the peppercorns are completely covered by the liquid. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the peppercorns release their active ingredients into the vodka. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the peppercorns. Transfer the peppercorn tincture to a smaller glass dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. When needed, take 20-30 drops of the tincture under the tongue or add it to a small amount of water or juice to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum, also known as black pepper, has a long history of helping people with motion sickness. The tincture made from black pepper seeds is especially useful for this problem. When you take the tincture, the active compound piperine goes into your body and helps prevent nausea and vomiting. This happens because piperine blocks the action of a chemical in your body that causes nausea. When you're on a ship, plane, or car and feel like you're going to be sick, the piperine in the tincture helps calm your stomach and makes you feel better. The tincture is also easy to take and can be mixed with water or other liquids, making it simple to use when you need it. People have been using black pepper for motion sickness for a long time, and it's becoming more popular as a natural and safe way to feel better when you're traveling.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal tincture for motion sickness, start by gathering fresh peppercorns, the dried seeds of the Piper nigrum plant. Crush the peppercorns to release their oils and then mix them with a neutral carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. The general ratio is one part crushed peppercorns to three parts carrier oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for two to four weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oils infuse into the carrier oil. After the steeping period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil. This oil can be taken sublingually - under the tongue - as needed to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal tinctures for motion sickness

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be an effective remedy for motion sickness, a condition that causes nausea, dizziness, and discomfort when traveling or engaging in activities that involve movement. Two herbs that have been traditionally used to alleviate motion sickness are Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, and Ginkgo biloba. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea, making it a popular choice for motion sickness. It also has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and disorientation. Ginkgo biloba, on the other hand, is known for its ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness by improving circulation and reducing swelling in the inner ear. By using herbal tinctures containing these herbs, individuals can alleviate their motion sickness symptoms and enjoy traveling or engaging in activities that they previously found uncomfortable or debilitating. Fixing motion sickness is important because it can significantly impact a person's quality of life, preventing them from participating in activities they enjoy or traveling to see loved ones. By alleviating motion sickness symptoms, individuals can improve their mental and physical well-being, allowing them to live a more active and fulfilling life.
supplementary_best: To remedy motion sickness, the best combination of herbal tinctures is Zingiber officinale (ginger) and Avena sativa (oat straw) together with a small amount of Valeriana officinalis (valerian root). Zingiber officinale is effective in calming the digestive system and reducing nausea, making it an ideal choice for motion sickness. Avena sativa, on the other hand, helps to balance the body's natural rhythms and can reduce anxiety and stress that often exacerbate motion sickness. Valeriana officinalis is added in small amounts to promote relaxation and reduce dizziness without causing drowsiness. This combination works well because it addresses multiple aspects of motion sickness: nausea, anxiety, and dizziness. Taking these tinctures before traveling, especially for a long duration or in areas prone to rough seas or roads, can help alleviate symptoms and reduce the risk of motion sickness.
supplementary_avoid: If you have motion sickness, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. Tinctures made from 'Hyoscyamus niger' (black henbane) should be avoided because they contain scopolamine, a substance that can cause dizziness and nausea. This can make motion sickness worse. Tinctures made from 'Datura stramonium' (jimsonweed) are also not recommended because they contain the same scopolamine compound, which can cause disorientation and worsen motion sickness symptoms.  Tinctures made from 'Atropa belladonna' (deadly nightshade) should be avoided as well because they contain atropine, a substance that can cause dizziness, nausea, and disorientation. These symptoms are the opposite of what you want if you have motion sickness. Tinctures made from 'Strychnos nux-vomica' (strychnine tree) should also be avoided because they contain strychnine, a substance that can cause intense nausea and vomiting, which can be triggered by motion sickness.  Tinctures made from 'Taxus brevifolia' (Pacific yew) should be avoided because they contain taxine, a substance that can cause nausea and vomiting. This can make motion sickness symptoms worse. It's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal tinctures, especially if you have a pre-existing medical condition like motion sickness.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger is a natural remedy that can help with motion sickness. It works by calming the stomach and reducing nausea. You can eat ginger in the form of candy, tea, or even fresh ginger. When you eat ginger, it helps to slow down the movement of food through your digestive system, which can help to prevent nausea. Some studies have shown that ginger is just as effective as some over-the-counter motion sickness medicines.  Another natural remedy that can help with motion sickness is peppermint oil. When you inhale peppermint oil, it helps to calm your stomach and reduce nausea. Peppermint oil works by slowing down the movement of food through your digestive system, which can help to prevent nausea. It also helps to relax your muscles, which can help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness.  Acupressure bands are also a natural remedy that can help with motion sickness. These bands work by applying pressure to a specific point on your wrist, which helps to calm your stomach and reduce nausea. This point, called the P6 or Nei Kuan point, is a spot on your wrist where the tendons and muscles meet. When you apply pressure to this point, it helps to stimulate your body's natural response to nausea, which can help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness.  Some people also find relief from motion sickness by getting some fresh air. When you get motion sickness, your body can become lightheaded and dizzy. Getting some fresh air can help to improve your circulation and reduce the symptoms of motion sickness. This is because fresh air contains oxygen, which helps to increase your circulation and reduce dizziness.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience motion sickness often have other health issues. One common problem is digestive issues, such as nausea and vomiting. The medicinal herb Ginger can help alleviate these symptoms. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the stomach and reduce nausea.  Another issue is fatigue and dizziness. Some people with motion sickness may experience low blood pressure, which can cause dizziness and lightheadedness. The herb Peppermint can help improve blood flow and alleviate dizziness. Peppermint's menthol content can also help to relax the muscles and calm the mind.  Motion sickness can also lead to anxiety and stress. The herb Lavender can help calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Lavender's calming properties can also help to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted in people with motion sickness.  Some people with motion sickness may experience headaches, often due to dehydration or tension. The herb Feverfew has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headaches. Feverfew can also help to reduce fever and improve digestion.  Lastly, motion sickness can cause inflammation and swelling in the body. The herb Turmeric contains a natural anti-inflammatory compound called Curcumin, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
